metadata.description: Azure region for all resources
Environment tag (dev | staging | prod)
Project name — used in all resource names
Owner alias — tagged on every resource
Object ID of your Azure AD user or service principal — grants Key Vault Secrets Officer so you can populate secrets after deploy. Leave empty to skip.
Kubernetes version to pin. Run: az aks get-versions -l eastus -o table
System node pool VM size
System node pool initial/minimum count

Enable cluster autoscaler on the system node pool — see modules/aks.bicep for why this must stay true (live config was set via Portal, not IaC, after a 2026-07-02 CPU exhaustion incident; redeploying with this false reverts it)
Autoscaler floor, only used when enableAutoScaling is true
Autoscaler ceiling, only used when enableAutoScaling is true
Maximum pods per node. Increase from Azure CNI default of 30 when running observability add-ons.
Log Analytics workspace name
Azure region for the workspace
Resource tags
Retention period in days (30–730)
Virtual network name
Azure region
Resource tags
VNet address space
AKS node subnet address prefix — must be large enough for node IPs with Azure CNI (/22 = ~1000 IPs)
Container Registry name — must be globally unique, alphanumeric, 5–50 chars
Azure region
Resource tags
SKU for the registry (Basic | Standard | Premium)
Key Vault name — 3–24 chars, alphanumeric + hyphens, globally unique
Azure region
Resource tags
Object ID of the identity that will administer the vault (e.g., your own user or a pipeline service principal)
AKS cluster name
Azure region
Resource tags
Resource ID of the AKS node subnet
Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Container Insights
Resource ID of the Azure Container Registry to attach
Resource ID of the Key Vault
Object ID of the admin user/service principal to grant AKS RBAC Cluster Admin. Leave empty to skip.
System node pool VM size
Initial/minimum system node count

Enable the cluster autoscaler on the system node pool. IMPORTANT: this was enabled manually via the Azure Portal on 2026-07-02 after a single-node CPU exhaustion incident (see docs/runbooks/phase4-federated-mcp.md), outside of Bicep. Keep this true and keep minNodeCount/maxNodeCount matching the live portal config — setting this false (the old default) and redeploying will silently scale the pool back down to a fixed `nodeCount` nodes and can starve pod scheduling again.
Autoscaler floor — only used when enableAutoScaling is true
Autoscaler ceiling — only used when enableAutoScaling is true
OS disk size in GB (0 = use managed default)
Maximum pods per node. Azure CNI default is 30 — increase for observability add-ons (cert-manager, prometheus, etc.)
Kubernetes version — check: az aks get-versions -l eastus -o table

agentPoolProfiles: [union(createObject('name', 'system', 'mode', 'System', 'count', parameters('nodeCount'), 'vmSize', parameters('nodeVmSize'), 'osDiskSizeGB', parameters('osDiskSizeGB'), 'osDiskType', 'Managed', 'osType', 'Linux', 'osSKU', 'AzureLinux', 'vnetSubnetID', parameters('aksSubnetId'), 'maxPods', parameters('maxPodsPerNode'), 'enableAutoScaling', parameters('enableAutoScaling'), 'type', 'VirtualMachineScaleSets', 'upgradeSettings', createObject('maxSurge', '1')), if(parameters('enableAutoScaling'), createObject('minCount', parameters('minNodeCount'), 'maxCount', parameters('maxNodeCount')), createObject()))]

metadata.description: Name of the user-assigned managed identity, e.g. id-github-mcp-server
Azure region
Resource tags
AKS OIDC issuer URL — from aks.bicep output oidcIssuerUrl
Kubernetes namespace of the workload ServiceAccount, e.g. mcp
Name of the Kubernetes ServiceAccount this identity federates with, e.g. github-mcp-server
Resource ID of the Key Vault to grant read access to
Name of the user-assigned managed identity, e.g. id-github-mcp-server
Azure region
Resource tags
AKS OIDC issuer URL — from aks.bicep output oidcIssuerUrl
Kubernetes namespace of the workload ServiceAccount, e.g. mcp
Name of the Kubernetes ServiceAccount this identity federates with, e.g. github-mcp-server
Resource ID of the Key Vault to grant read access to
Resource group that contains all ContextForge resources
AKS cluster name — pass to: az aks get-credentials -g <rg> -n <cluster>
ACR login server — used in image pull and Helm values
ACR name
Key Vault name — populate secrets here before running helm-aks
Key Vault URI
OIDC issuer URL — needed for workload identity federation
CSI driver managed identity object ID — already granted Key Vault Secrets User
GitHub MCP workload identity client ID — use in the ServiceAccount azure.workload.identity/client-id annotation and the SecretProviderClass clientID
Azure DevOps MCP workload identity client ID — use in the ServiceAccount azure.workload.identity/client-id annotation and the SecretProviderClass clientID
